primary.name: Mid-Century Museum Retrospective Exhibition Design
primary.description: Inspired by chronologically-organized museum exhibitions where visitors walk through sequential rooms, each representing a different era or theme. The vertical timeline navigation mimics the experience of moving through gallery spaces in order, with each room telling a distinct chapter of the story.
elements_borrowed: Sequential room numbering with Roman numerals
Museum-quality card presentations with generous spacing
Docent-like descriptive prose for each view section
Timeline spine as the primary organizational metaphor

secondary.name: Charles and Ray Eames timeline exhibitions
secondary.description: The Eames Office is known for creating timeline-based exhibitions that present complex information in a linear, digestible format. Their approach to mixing photography, typography, and objects in a timeline format informs the alternating left-right content layout.
elements_borrowed: Alternating side presentation of content nodes
Clean serif typography for exhibit labels
Information hierarchy through size and position

secondary.name: Vitra Design Museum exhibition catalogs
secondary.description: The Vitra Design Museum's approach to cataloging furniture design history influenced the card-based content presentation and the way each project, idea, or entity is treated as a museum specimen worthy of examination.
elements_borrowed: Specimen card layouts with label/title/body structure
Generous white space between displayed items

secondary.name: Vertical timeline navigation patterns
secondary.description: Vertical dot-based timeline navigation seen in modern web portfolios and annual report sites, adapted here as a right-rail navigation system where each dot represents an exhibit room.
elements_borrowed: Connected dot navigation with spine line

differentiators: Pass 8 uses right-rail timeline nav instead of horizontal shelf
Pass 8 uses DM Serif Display instead of Playfair Display
Pass 8 uses museum docent persona instead of warm workspace
Pass 8 uses timeline-alternating content flow instead of card grid
Pass 8 uses slide-up cascade motion instead of subtle ease transitions

Pass 8 uses right-rail nav instead of orbital ring navigation
Pass 8 uses DM Serif Display + Lato instead of Playfair Display + DM Sans
Pass 8 uses amber/terracotta palette instead of warm white + orange
Pass 8 uses sequential room metaphor instead of central hub metaphor
Pass 8 uses vertical timeline dots instead of pill toggle group
Pass 8 uses timeline-alternating flow instead of stacked-card carousel
Pass 8 uses scroll-snap-vertical instead of swipe-horizontal
Pass 8 uses DM Serif Display instead of Fraunces
Pass 8 uses muted terracotta palette instead of bold textile colors

Pass 8 uses timeline-alternating instead of editorial columns
Pass 8 uses slide-up cascade instead of cinematic fade
Pass 8 uses DM Serif Display instead of Cormorant Garamond

Pass 8 uses vertical dots instead of pill icons
Pass 8 uses slide-up cascade instead of spring bounce
Pass 8 uses DM Serif Display instead of Lora

Pass 8 uses balanced density instead of compact

uniquenessVerification.shellMode: right-rail-nav — NOT used in any previous pass (P1: top shelf, P2: radial hub, P3: floating pill, P4: hidden hamburger, P5: bottom dock, P6: left sidebar)
uniquenessVerification.navPattern: vertical-dots-timeline — NOT used in any previous pass (P1: icon-label row, P2: orbital ring, P3: pill toggle, P4: fullscreen overlay, P5: icon pill dock, P6: accordion)
uniquenessVerification.contentFlow: timeline-alternating — NOT used in any previous pass (P1: card grid, P2: radial sectors, P3: stacked carousel, P4: editorial columns, P5: centered single panel, P6: split panel detail)
uniquenessVerification.typography: DM Serif Display + Lato + IBM Plex Mono — unique combination not used in any previous pass
uniquenessVerification.colorPalette: bg #f8f3ea, accent #b8632e, accent2 #3a7c5f — distinct from all previous pass palettes
uniquenessVerification.motionLanguage: slide-up-cascade — NOT used in any previous pass (P1: subtle ease, P2: orbital float, P3: card-flip-slide, P4: cinematic-fade, P5: spring-bounce, P6: collapse-expand)
